title: The Signs Before the End
subtitle: On the Mount of Olives, facing the doomed Temple, the Lord foretells the signs of His coming

stillness: Of the day He does not speak; of the signs alone He teaches.
For uncertainty of the time brings holy dread,
and dread makes ready the soul.
Be still, then, and let the fear of His coming prepare you.
gospel: And Jesus, going out of the temple, was walking away.
And His disciples came to Him to show Him the buildings of the temple.
But He answering said to them:
Do you see all these things?
Amen I say to you, there shall not be left here a stone upon a stone that shall not be destroyed.
And as He sat upon the Mount of Olives, over against the temple,
the disciples came to Him secretly, saying:
Tell us, when shall these things be?
And what shall be the sign of Thy coming, and of the consummation of the world?
And Jesus answering said to them: Take heed that no man seduce you.
For you shall hear of wars, and rumours of wars.
Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom;
and there shall be earthquakes in places,
and pestilences, and famines, and terrors from heaven, and great signs.
behold: Behold the Lord Jesus going forth from the temple that same third day,
signifying by His very going out what He had spoken:
that the house of the Jews must be forsaken.
See the disciples, marvelling that a temple of such beauty and strength should be destroyed and brought to nothing,
pointing out to Him the buildings, not unknown to Him, but as if to be wondered at —
looking not to the holiness of the temple, but to the costliness and might of its stones.
See how even today many attend more to the bodily worship of the Church than to the spiritual.
Behold the Lord seated upon the Mount of Olives, over against the temple,
in the place where afterward a church was built,
for from that place the temple and the city are seen most clearly;
and there too the last judgment shall come to pass.
Consider that here, fittingly, the disciples ask Him of the things to come.
consider: There shall not be left a stone upon a stone, however precious and strong it seemed to them.
This was done by the Romans in the forty-second year after the Lord's Passion.
The shadow is taken away, and truth, declared through the world, holds the palm.
The bodily and figural things have fallen, that the spiritual and true mysteries might rise.
Take heed that no man seduce you — that no one lead you aside from the truth of the faith.
For many false christs and false prophets shall come in My name,
saying: I am Christ — and shall seduce many,
by false doctrine, by feigned life, by wondrous signs.
In every heresy the devil, in disguise, says: I am Christ, and with me is the truth.
Christ shall not come unless the signs He foretold go before.
These He foretells to us, to call us back from the love of the world,
to strike fear and dread into us,
and to find us ever watchful and ready;
for the darts that are foreseen wound far less.
Consider the seven evils that go before His coming:
the enmities of peoples, for nation shall rise against nation;
the discord of princes, for kingdom against kingdom;
the shaking of places, for there shall be earthquakes;
the infections of sickness, for there shall be pestilences;
the failing of food, for there shall be famines;
the terror of heaven's signs, for there shall be terrors from above;
the strangeness of unheard-of things, for great signs shall come to pass.
It is fitting that these things first be done, as ordained by God:
for the trial and profit of the good, and the destruction of the wicked,
that those who proudly fought against the Author of all things may be assailed by all things,
and those who offended in all, may in all be smitten.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, Judge of all who art to come,
grant me constancy in the truth of the faith,
that I be not seduced by feigned and false men,
nor sink down beneath my tribulations;
but in the confession of Thy name, and in the assertion of the truth,
may I be able to stand firm with strength,
and for this to bear gladly the hatred of men and all adversity.
Grant me the virtue of patience,
that, though iniquity abound, the ardour of charity grow not cold in me;
but that, even to the end of this present life,
I may persevere in the truth of the faith and in the love of goodness,
so that, by Thy mercy, I may be saved,
O Lord God of my salvation. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Aufertur ergo umbra, et palmam tenet veritas per orbem declarata.
latinJewel.english: The shadow, then, is taken away, and truth, declared throughout the world, holds the palm of victory.
